title: WOS Workflow
description: Single author-time schema for a WOS workflow. Scales from Forms+ intake (~30 lines) to complex case management (~3000 lines) without changing shape. Required core: impactLevel, actors, lifecycle. Optional embedded blocks (governance, agents, aiOversight, signature, custody, advanced, assurance) appear only when product behavior demands them. Conditional rules require governance for rightsImpacting/safetyImpacting workflows, agents block for agent-typed actors, and signature block for signature-gated transitions. Deployment-environment configuration (calendars, notification templates, ontology alignment) lives in two sidecars referenced by targetWorkflow URI. See ADR 0076.
$comment: Promotion status (PLN-0314): top-level core leaves carry full normative description + examples + x-lm.critical annotations on load-bearing nodes. Inner-block leaves under governance/agents/aiOversight/signature/custody/advanced/assurance remain sketch-quality pending the spec absorption pass (PLN-0176..0207); the canonical descriptions for those leaves are owned by kernel/spec.md, governance/spec.md, ai/ai-integration.md, and advanced/spec.md and absorb here once those §-numbered sections land. SCHEMA-DOC-001 violations under inner blocks are tracked, not hallucinated. The six canonical kernel extension seams (ADR 0077) — actorExtension, contractHook, provenanceLayer, lifecycleHook, custodyHook, extensions/x- keys — remain authoritative; new kernel extension points MUST attach to one of these or be ratified via a new ADR amending Kernel §10. Step 5 ($defs promotion) landed: FactsTierRecord, MutationSource, VerificationLevel, CaseFileSnapshot, CapabilityInvocationRecord live here (plus transitive ProvenanceOutcome); the runtime log schema (wos-provenance-log.schema.json) $refs them at https://wos-spec.org/schemas/wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/<Name> and layers runtime-record-shape allOf entries (CaseCreated/IntakeAccepted/IntakeRejected/IntakeDeferred/SignatureAffirmation) on top.

$wosWorkflow.description: Sole top-level version marker that identifies this object as a WOS Workflow Document and pins it to the 1.0 line of the merged-schema product-tier model (ADR 0076). Stream identity (governance, agents, signature, custody, advanced) is implicit in the envelope version; compliance claims compose as $wosWorkflow@X.Y. Replaces the historical four-document markers $wosKernel / $wosWorkflowGovernance / $wosAIIntegration / $wosAdvanced.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker and version pin for WOS Workflow documents per ADR 0076

url.description: Canonical URI identifier for this Workflow Document. The URL names the workflow definition, not any particular instance — all versions of the same workflow SHOULD share this URL, and the pair (url, version) SHOULD be globally unique. Sidecars (`wos-delivery`, `wos-ontology-alignment`) join by this URL via `targetWorkflow`. Prefer stable, resolvable URIs under a namespace the authoring organization controls.

x-lm.intent: Stable identifier of the workflow across versions; sidecars join here

version.description: Version string for this Workflow Document. Semantic Versioning (SemVer 2.0.0) is RECOMMENDED so processors and governance layers can reason about compatibility. Instance versioning (Kernel §9.6) pins each running workflow instance to the (url, version) pair of the definition that created it.

title.description: Short human-readable name for this workflow, suitable for lists, navigation, and audit summaries. Not interpreted by the processor.

description.description: Free-text description of this workflow's purpose, scope, and intended audience. Consumed by documentation tooling and LLM authoring agents; not interpreted by the processor.
examples: Internal workflow for approving purchase orders under $50k; routed to a manager for approval or rejection.
Medicaid eligibility redetermination with appeal branch; rightsImpacting, requires full due process.

status.description: Publication status of this workflow definition. `draft` means the definition is being authored and SHOULD NOT instantiate production cases. `active` means the definition is in effect and new instances MAY be created. `deprecated` means no new instances SHOULD be created, though existing pinned instances continue executing.

impactLevel.description: Consequence level classification for decisions made within this workflow (Kernel §6). `rightsImpacting`: decisions affect individual legal rights — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `safetyImpacting`: decisions affect safety — full due process REQUIRED (governance block MUST be present). `operational`: organizational operations — governance RECOMMENDED. `informational`: no binding decisions — governance OPTIONAL. Drives conditional block requirements via the schema's allOf rules.

x-lm.intent: Proportionality index that governs the strength of required controls across all embedded blocks

caseFile.description: Structured case data for instances of this workflow: either inline `fields` (FieldDeclaration map) or a pinned `contractRef` (Formspec Definition recommended). Mutually exclusive shapes per CaseFile oneOf.

x-lm.intent: Structured case data shape — either inline field declaration or Formspec contract reference

actors.description: Non-empty list of actors (humans, systems, or agents) that may perform transitions. Each entry has a unique `id`; transitions reference these ids. When any actor uses type `agent`, the `agents` and `aiOversight` blocks are required (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001).

x-lm.intent: Who can act in this workflow — humans, systems, or agents (the agent type is gated by AI Integration block presence per ADR 0076 D-2; cross-ref enforced by lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001)

lifecycle.description: Author-time state machine: `initialState` plus a map of state identifiers to State declarations (atomic, compound, parallel, foreach, or final). Topology is immutable for a pinned (url, version); runtime instances follow this graph.

x-lm.intent: State machine spine — initial state + lifecycle topology that all other concerns attach to

governance.description: Embedded Layer-1 governance (due process, review protocols, validation pipelines, policy parameters) inside the author-time workflow envelope. Required for rightsImpacting and safetyImpacting impactLevel per kernel and ADR 0076 D-2.

x-lm.intent: Embedded due-process / review / pipeline governance — REQUIRED for rightsImpacting/safetyImpacting workflows per ADR 0076 D-2

agents.description: Per-agent declarations (model, autonomy, deontic constraints, drift policy) embedded in the merged workflow. Required when any actor has type agent (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001).

x-lm.intent: Per-agent declarations — REQUIRED when any actor has type='agent' (lint WOS-AGENT-XREF-001 enforces the cross-reference)

aiOversight.description: Cross-cutting AI oversight embedded in the workflow envelope: disclosure posture, drift detection, narrative tier handling, volume constraints. Pairs with the agents array (ADR 0076).

x-lm.intent: Cross-cutting AI policy — disclosure (EU AI Act Art. 13, OMB M-24-10), drift detection, narrative tier; pairs with agents block

signature.description: Embedded DocuSign-tier signature profile (roles, documents, signingFlow, evidence) inside the author-time envelope. Required when transitions gate on kind signature (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001).

x-lm.intent: Embedded signature workflow — LOAD-BEARING for DocuSign-tier (signing order IS the workflow); REQUIRED when any transition gates on kind='signature' (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001 enforces signer coverage)

custody.description: Embedded Trellis custody binding (trust profile, anchor requirements) for workflows that claim anchoring on transitions or signature events.

x-lm.intent: Trellis custody binding — load-bearing whenever a workflow claims anchoring on transitions or signature events

extensions.description: Vendor-namespaced extension container realizing Kernel §10.6 (the `extensions` seam — one of the canonical six per ADR 0077). Every key MUST start with `x-`; the prefix `x-wos-` is RESERVED for future normative WOS use (lint K-EXT-002 warns). Values are opaque to the processor; vendors document their own subkeys.

$schema.description: Standard JSON Schema annotation declaring the meta-schema URI. Not interpreted by the WOS processor; consumed by IDEs/validators for tooling integration.

execution.description: Execution configuration (Kernel §9). Workflow/task/service timeouts, compensability, and instance-versioning policy. Honored by the durable runtime adapter; spec-authoritative semantics live in `wos-runtime`.

compensable.description: Whether the workflow scope is compensable (Kernel §9.5).

evaluationMode.description: Evaluation mode for guards and computed expressions (Kernel §12). `event-driven` (default) re-evaluates on transition events; `continuous` re-evaluates on any case-file mutation.

allOf.title: Rights-impacting and safety-impacting workflows MUST embed governance.
Workflows whose actors include any agent-typed actor MUST declare agents[].

allOf.$comment: Cross-reference rule beyond JSON Schema expressivity: every actor with type=='agent' MUST have a matching agents[].id. Enforced by lint rule WOS-AGENT-XREF-001.

Cross-cutting rule beyond JSON Schema expressivity: any transition gated by signature (on.kind=='signature') requires a signature block, and signers[] MUST cover the gating actor. Enforced by lint rule WOS-SIG-COVER-001.

allOf.title: Workflows with both agents and advanced features SHOULD declare aiOversight.

CaseFile.description: Structured case data. Either declared inline via fields, or referenced via contractRef. Formspec is the recommended contract binding (kernel contractHook seam).

fields.description: Inline field declarations for the case data shape. Each entry's key is the field name and value is a `FieldDeclaration` describing type and constraints. Use `fields` for self-contained workflows; use `contractRef` to reference an external contract (Formspec recommended).

contractRef.description: URI reference to an external contract document defining the case data shape. Recommended binding is a Formspec Definition (per the canonical `contractHook` seam, ADR 0077 §10.2); JSON Schema is the baseline binding. Mutually exclusive with inline `fields`.

contractVersion.description: Optional version string pin for the referenced contract. When omitted, processors resolve the latest published version of `contractRef`. Pinning is RECOMMENDED for case instances that must replay against archived semantics (Kernel §9.6).

required.description: When true, the field MUST be present and non-null at validation time. When omitted or false, the field is optional. Enforcement layer is the contract validator bound by `contractHook`.

description.description: Free-text description of the field's purpose, displayed to authoring tools and rendered into LLM-readable artifacts. Not interpreted by the processor; the field's semantic meaning lives in the bound contract.
examples: The applicant's full legal name as it appears on government-issued ID.
Monthly gross income in USD, before taxes.

FieldDeclaration.description: Author-time field declaration in a contract intake binding (Kernel §9.1). Pairs a field name with its primitive type and optional documentation; the bound contract carries the semantic meaning.

IntakeReference.description: Public-intake form. References a Formspec definition. Validated intake is converted to caseFile via IntakeHandoff (ADR 0073).

contractRef.description: URI reference to the Formspec Definition that defines the public-intake form. Validated intake responses are converted to `caseFile` via `IntakeHandoff` per ADR 0073. Required for any workflow that accepts public intake.

contractVersion.description: Optional version pin for the intake form contract. When omitted, processors resolve latest. Pinning is RECOMMENDED for SBA pilot deployments to keep production replay deterministic.

id.description: Stable identifier for this actor within the workflow document. MUST be unique across actors[]. Cross-references include `Transition.actor` (who acts on the transition) and, when type=='agent', `agents[].id`. Identifier convention: camelCase or kebab-case strings; no whitespace.

type.$comment: Kernel: 'human' | 'system'. AI Integration adds 'agent' via actorExtension seam (ADR 0077). Open via x- prefix.
type.description: Actor classification. Kernel layer recognizes 'human' and 'system'; the AI Integration layer adds 'agent' via the `actorExtension` seam (ADR 0077). Vendor-defined types ride on `x-` prefixed values per §10.6.

role.description: Optional human-readable role label for documentation and notice rendering. Not interpreted by the processor; the actor identity for execution and authority decisions is `id`, not `role`.

states.description: Map of state identifier to State declaration. Defines the workflow's complete topology: atomic states (one transition target per event), compound states (nested substates), parallel states (concurrent regions), foreach states (bounded iteration per ADR 0078), and final states.

initialState.description: Identifier of the lifecycle's initial state — the state instances enter on creation. MUST match a key in `states`. Per Kernel §4.1, every workflow has exactly one initial state.

items.description: Semantic tag string attaching governance and policy to this state. Governance attaches via the `lifecycleHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.4) by matching tags. Common tags: `determination`, `adverse-decision`, `hold`, `appeal`, `terminal`. Custom tags MUST use kebab-case; vendor-specific tags use `x-` prefix.

states.description: Nested substates for compound states (`type=='compound'`). Map of substate identifier to State declaration. Compound states enter their declared `initial` substate; history states (Kernel §4.14) optionally restore the most recently active substate on re-entry.

regions.description: Parallel regions for parallel states (`type=='parallel'`). Map of region identifier to a region object containing its own `initial` and `states`. All regions execute concurrently; the parallel state exits only when join semantics declared by `cancellationPolicy` are satisfied (Kernel §4.8).

onEntry.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute on state entry, before any transitions are evaluated. Action shape follows Kernel §9.2 action execution model (invokeService, startTimer, emitEvent, etc.). Actions execute in declaration order; the state is considered entered only after all onEntry actions succeed.

onExit.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute on state exit, after the outgoing transition has been selected but before the next state is entered. Same action shape as onEntry. Exit actions cannot abort the transition; they execute as side effects.

stateTimeout.description: ISO 8601 duration after which the processor emits a `$timeout.state` event if the state has not been exited. The lifecycle handles the timeout like any other event (Kernel §4.10). Hold policies (Governance §12) MAY override stateTimeout with policy-specific durations.

collection.$comment: Foreach only. FEL expression yielding a bounded array. ADR 0078.
collection.description: Foreach states only (`type=='foreach'`). FEL expression yielding a bounded array. The processor evaluates the expression at state entry, instantiates `body` once per item, and aggregates per `mergeStrategy`. Per ADR 0078, the array MUST be bounded — unbounded collections are rejected.

itemVariable.description: Foreach states only. Variable name bound to the current item within `body`. Defaults to `$item`. Referenced in body actions and guards as `$item.fieldName` or whatever name is declared.

indexVariable.description: Foreach states only. Variable name bound to the current item index (0-based) within `body`. Defaults to `$index`. Useful for ordered processing or correlation with parallel arrays.

concurrency.description: Foreach states only. Maximum number of items processed concurrently. Integer for bounded concurrency; null for unbounded (processor decides). Per ADR 0078, processors MAY refuse unbounded concurrency on rights-impacting workflows.

breakCondition.description: Foreach states only. FEL expression evaluated after each iteration; when true, terminates the foreach early. Useful for early-exit on first match or threshold reached.

outputPath.description: Foreach states only. Case-file path where iteration results are written per `mergeStrategy`. Per ADR 0078, the write goes through the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080) with `mutationSource: computed`.

outcomeCode.description: Final states only (`type=='final'`). Stable code identifying the workflow's terminal outcome. Recorded in Facts-tier provenance and consumed by reporting / case-closure pipelines. Distinct from `tags` (which compose) — outcomeCode is singular.

guard.description: FEL expression evaluated before the transition fires. When the guard returns true, the transition is selected; when it returns false, the transition is skipped. When the guard throws or evaluates to a non-boolean, the processor MUST reject per Kernel §4.6/§4.7 transition resolution. Guards are stateless — they read case state but MUST NOT mutate it. The compiled FEL AST drives lint analysis (K-012 syntax, K-017 cross-case-state reference, K-019 function-set, AI-058 boolean shape).

x-lm.intent: Where every conditional logic decision lives — the highest-traffic FEL surface, lint-checked for syntax / cross-state / function-set / boolean-shape correctness

items.description: Semantic tag string attaching governance and policy to this transition. Governance attaches via the `lifecycleHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.4) by matching tags. Common tags: `determination`, `adverse-decision`, `appeal-grant`, `correction`, `rescission`.

actor.description: Actor identifier responsible for performing this transition. MUST match an entry in the workflow's `actors[].id`. Single-actor transitions name one actor; multi-actor (signature-gated) transitions use the `actors` array (lint WOS-SIG-COVER-001 enforces signer coverage). Load-bearing for authority decisions and signature workflow correctness.

x-lm.intent: Who acts on the transition — load-bearing for authority decisions, signature coverage (WOS-SIG-COVER-001), and audit traceability

actions.description: Ordered list of actions the processor MUST execute as part of this transition, after guard evaluation and before destination state entry. Action shape per Kernel §9.2. Actions execute atomically with the state change — failure rolls back per Kernel §9.5 compensation.

target.description: Identifier of the destination state. MUST match a key in the enclosing lifecycle's states (or a substate when nested). The processor MUST reject transitions whose `to` does not resolve.

name.description: Optional event name for kinds that carry a name (signal, message). For typed events ($timeout.*, $error, $join), the kind discriminator carries the meaning and `name` MAY be omitted. Kernel §4.5 typed-event vocabulary applies.

actorId.description: Optional actor identifier filter. When present, the event matches only when the dispatched event's source actor matches this id. Useful for routing distinct actor actions through the same event name.

correlationKey.description: Optional correlation key for matching async events back to their originating transition. Used by the integration profile's CloudEvents extension attributes (Kernel §9.2 — woscausationeventid / woscorrelationkey) to bind callbacks to their request context.

signalTimeout.description: Signal events only. ISO 8601 duration after which the awaited signal expires and a `$timeout.signal` event fires instead. Composes with hold policies (Governance §12) on hold-tagged states.

eventContract.description: Optional URI of the contract that validates the event's payload. Per Kernel §9.2, eventContract enables typed event payloads with schema-enforced shape. Formspec contract recommended; JSON Schema is baseline.

Contracts.$comment: Validation/guard contracts and capability contracts. Pairs with bindings[] per ADR 0080 governed output-commit pipeline.
Contracts.description: Named validation and capability contracts referenced by lifecycle transitions, output bindings, and the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Contracts compose with the `contractHook` seam (ADR 0077 §10.2). Concrete shape varies by binding kind; see kernel/spec.md §9.2 for the merged contract surface.

OutputBinding.description: Governed output-commit pipeline projection. Per ADR 0080: source surface + contractRef + projection map + writeScope + mutationSource + verificationLevel. Validated mutations + Facts-tier provenance emit deterministically.

on.$comment: Source surface: state id, transition id, capability id, signal name.
on.description: Source surface that triggers this binding. Identifier of a state, transition, capability, or signal whose completion projects mutations through the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). The binding fires when the source surface emits its terminal event.

contractRef.description: URI of the contract that validates the projected mutation before commit. Per ADR 0080's six-surface pipeline, every binding MUST reference a contract; the validator gates the commit. Formspec contract recommended for case-data writes.

projection.description: Map of source-output keys to case-file field paths. Defines how the source surface's output projects into the case state. Concrete shape is binding-specific; for Formspec bindings, projection follows the Mapping spec.

items.description: Field-path expression naming a case-file location this binding is permitted to mutate. The processor MUST reject mutations outside writeScope at commit time. Per ADR 0080 D-2, writeScope is the load-bearing authorization claim.

mutationSource.description: Provenance origin of the projected mutation under the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Recorded in Facts-tier provenance (Kernel §8); load-bearing for audit traceability and rights-impacting decision review. The closed-set values describe canonical provenance origins; vendor-namespaced `x-` values cover origins outside the canonical set.

x-lm.intent: Where the mutation came from — load-bearing audit-traceability claim per ADR 0080 governed output-commit pipeline

oneOf.description: Vendor-namespaced mutation source identifier under the `x-` prefix per Kernel §10.6 (`extensions` seam). Used when none of the canonical mutation sources (agent-extracted, system-fetched, human-entered, human-corrected, computed, self-attested) describes the actual provenance.

verificationLevel.description: Strength of verification applied to the projected mutation under the governed output-commit pipeline (ADR 0080). Higher levels increase audit weight and enable stricter governance gates (e.g., `authoritative` defeats lower-tier challenges in ADR 0066 amendment/supersession evaluation). The closed-set values describe canonical verification regimes; vendor-namespaced `x-` values cover bespoke regimes (e.g., FHE-attested, dual-control). Lint WOS-VER-LEVEL-001 warns when `agents[].fallbackChain` is declared without any `verificationLevel` on the workflow.

x-lm.intent: How strongly verified the mutation is — load-bearing for governance gating and fallback-chain integrity (lint WOS-VER-LEVEL-001)

oneOf.description: Vendor-namespaced verification level identifier under the `x-` prefix per Kernel §10.6 (`extensions` seam). Used when none of the canonical levels (independent, attested, corroborated, authoritative) describes the binding's verification regime.

tier.description: Minimum provenance tier emitted. Facts is kernel default. Higher tiers attach via provenanceLayer seam (ADR 0077).

Governance.description: Embedded due-process, review, and pipeline governance. Required for rightsImpacting and safetyImpacting impactLevel. Was wos-workflow-governance.schema.json plus wos-policy-parameters.schema.json plus wos-due-process.schema.json plus wos-assertion-gate.schema.json.

dueProcess.$comment: Notice, opportunity to be heard, appeal mechanism, adverse-decision policy.

policyParameters.$comment: Temporal parameters, deadline config, business calendar refs.

amendmentTaxonomy.description: Five-mode amendment taxonomy declared by this workflow's governance, per ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26 maximalist closed-five position. Closed enum -- vendor extensions are deliberately not permitted at this key so the lint that pairs each declared mode with its authorizing-act record kind (correctionAuthorized, amendmentAuthorized, rescissionAuthorized, reinstated; supersession is operationalized via caseRelationship.type="supersedes") stays exhaustive. Authoring this enum makes the lint cheaper to land in follow-up work; absence here means the workflow asserts no amendments are permitted.

x-lm.intent: Closed five-mode amendment taxonomy per ADR-0066 D-1 / Q26 maximalist; lint target for taxonomy-mode ↔ record-kind pairing

AgentDeclaration.description: Was wos-ai-integration.schema.json AgentDeclaration plus wos-agent-config.schema.json plus wos-drift-monitor.schema.json. Inlined per ADR 0076.

id.$comment: MUST match an actor.id where actor.type=='agent'.

autonomy.$comment: Capped by impactLevel. rightsImpacting MUST NOT exceed assistive without explicit governance permission.

fallbackChain.$comment: Actor ids, in order. MUST terminate in a human-review actor for rightsImpacting transitions.

capabilities.$comment: Capability sub-shape: declared write scope, contract refs, tool grants.

AIOversight.description: Cross-cutting AI policy. Disclosure, drift detection, narrative provenance, volume constraints. EU AI Act Art. 13 / OMB M-24-10 alignment.

Signature.description: Signature content embedded in a $wosWorkflow document (Kernel §13 / T4 Signature Profile). Carries roles, signable documents, signing flow, evidence requirements, authentication policies, and policy hooks (reminders, expiry, decline, void, reassignment). Spec authority: `wos-runtime/src/runtime/signature.rs::SignatureProfileDocument`.

targetWorkflow.description: Target workflow declaration for cross-artifact Signature Profiles.

roles.description: Signature role declarations binding signing roles to kernel actors.

signingFlow.description: Signing flow declaration: sequential, parallel, routed, or free-for-all.

guard.description: FEL guard expression for routed steps.

evidence.description: Signature evidence requirements emitted into SignatureAffirmation provenance.

authenticationPolicies.description: Authentication policy declarations referenced by role.authenticationPolicyKey.

reminders.description: Reminder policy emitting timer-driven events while signing is pending.

schedule.description: ISO 8601 durations relative to signing-flow start.

auditCertificate.description: Audit certificate emission policy. Captures what the certificate carries when issued.

voidPolicy.description: Void policy declaring who may void the signing flow and whether a reason is required.

Custody.description: Embedded Trellis custody declaration. Load-bearing whenever a workflow claims anchoring on transitions or signature events. Was wos-custody-hook-encoding.schema.json (sidecar); promoted to embedded per ADR 0076 D-2 / Q4.

on.$comment: Transition id, signature event id, or determination tag.

Advanced.description: Optional. DCR constraint zones, equity guardrails, SMT verifiable constraints, multi-step session config, circuit breakers. Was wos-advanced.schema.json plus wos-equity.schema.json plus wos-verification-report.schema.json (author-time half).

equity.$comment: Bias caps, disparity thresholds, equity FEL expressions.

verifiableConstraints.$comment: SMT constraint declarations. Verification report is a runtime artifact in wos-provenance-log.

Assurance.description: Optional. Assurance level declaration, attestation, subject continuity. Was wos-assurance.schema.json.

FactsTierRecord.description: A persisted WOS provenance record emitted by runtime execution. Facts-tier semantics are normative in the kernel; higher tiers reuse the same envelope and advertise their tier through `auditLayer`. State transition records whose transitionTags include 'determination' MUST include a caseFileSnapshot so later notices, appeals, and override reviews can reproduce the exact factual basis used by the decision.

id.description: TypeID-structured identifier for this facts-tier provenance record. Reserved family prefix: `prov`.

recordKind.description: Provenance record discriminator. For determination snapshot enforcement, the relevant value is 'stateTransition'. Other record kinds MAY also carry caseFileSnapshot for implementation-specific diagnostics.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp stamped by the runtime append path before persistence.

actorId.description: Identifier of the actor who triggered the record, when the processor can name one.

fromState.description: Kernel lifecycle state label naming the configuration leaf immediately before this transition-oriented provenance record was emitted.

toState.description: Kernel lifecycle state label naming the configuration leaf immediately after this transition-oriented provenance record was emitted.

event.description: Triggering event or reserved event literal carried by the record kind.

data.description: Arbitrary JSON object carrying record-kind-specific correlation keys, diagnostics, or structured details not represented by top-level scalar provenance fields.

auditLayer.description: Audit tier for the persisted/exported record. Facts-tier records are normative in the kernel; higher layers reuse the same envelope and set a different tier literal.

actorType.description: Actor classification when the processor can resolve one canonically. Reserved literals are `human`, `system`, and `agent`; extension values MUST use an `x-` prefix.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state at the time of the action when the processor can determine it canonically.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document stamped at append time.

inputs.description: Input entity references used by this activity.

items.description: Opaque or resolvable token naming an input entity this activity consumed, aligned with PROV-O `prov:used` semantics for downstream audit graph materialization.

outputs.description: Output entity references generated by this activity.

items.description: Opaque or resolvable token naming an output entity this activity produced so exporters can stitch forward references into case files or external systems.

transitionTags.description: Semantic tags copied from the firing transition when this record represents a state transition. The presence of 'determination' makes caseFileSnapshot mandatory.

items.description: One semantic transition tag copied from the Kernel transition definition, such as 'determination' for consequential decisions or 'review' for human review steps.

mutationSource.description: Origin of the mutation when recordKind is 'caseStateMutation'. OPTIONAL on other record kinds. Reserved literals trace the provenance chain from human entry through AI extraction to system-fetch and computed derivation.

verificationLevel.description: OPTIONAL verification strength of this mutation. When present, signals the degree of independent confirmation behind the written value. Tying this to 'determination'-tagged transitions is policy-shaped (governance profile), not a blanket kernel MUST.

canonicalEventHash.description: Lowercase hex rendering of the 32-byte Trellis `canonical_event_hash` stamped after custody admission, when present.

MutationSource.description: Origin of a case-state mutation (Kernel S5.4). Open enum: the listed values are reserved; vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix. `agent-extracted` supersedes the FlowSpec `ai-extracted` literal for WOS actor-vocabulary alignment.

VerificationLevel.description: Verification strength of a case-state mutation (Kernel S5.4). Open enum: the listed values are reserved; vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix. OPTIONAL on mutation records; tying it to `determination`-tagged transitions is policy-shaped (governance profile / attachment pattern).

CaseFileSnapshot.description: Canonical case-file snapshot captured immediately before a determination-tagged transition fires. The value is the factual input to the determination; jcsCanonical and sha256 make the snapshot stable and tamper-evident for notices, appeals, and audit export.

value.description: Raw case-file JSON value observed immediately before the determination transition fires. This value may be an object, array, string, number, boolean, or null depending on the processor's case-state representation.

jcsCanonical.description: Canonical JSON string for value using JCS (RFC 8785) canonicalization semantics. Identical case-file state MUST produce byte-identical canonical strings.

sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 hex digest of jcsCanonical. This digest links the human-readable snapshot to tamper-evident exports without requiring downstream systems to reserialize value.

CapabilityInvocationRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted by the AI §3.3.1 capability-invocation path. A record representing a precondition-blocked invocation (`recordKind == "capabilityInvocation"` AND `data.invocationBlocked == true`) MUST carry `outcome: "preconditionNotSatisfied"` so audit tooling can distinguish a declarative precondition gate from an agent failure. Every provenance log validated against this kernel schema participates in the MUST via `FactsTierRecord.allOf`; records with other `recordKind` values or absent `data.invocationBlocked` fall outside the guard and are unaffected.

recordKind.description: Provenance record discriminator. `capabilityInvocation` selects records emitted by the AI §3.3.1 capability-invocation path (whether the invocation proceeded, was blocked by a precondition, or failed post-invocation).

data.description: Record payload for a capability-invocation provenance entry. Carries the AI §3.3.1 `invocationBlocked` flag plus any additional context the processor chooses to record (capability id, resolved precondition value, expression source, etc.).

invocationBlocked.description: True when the processor declined to invoke the agent because a capability precondition evaluated to `false` or a non-boolean (AI Integration §3.3.1). The processor MUST then fall through to the fallback chain (AI §8). False (or absent) means the capability was invoked.

x-lm.intent: Signal that a capability precondition blocked agent invocation; pairs with outcome=preconditionNotSatisfied to distinguish declarative gates from agent failures

AuthorityBasis.description: Authority-evidence reference shared by ADR-0066 amendment-taxonomy authorizing acts (correctionAuthorized, amendmentAuthorized, rescissionAuthorized, reinstated, authorizationAttestation) and ADR-0071 migration pin changes. Discriminated union: `kind: "uri"` carries an external policy/statute URI in `value`; `kind: "actorPolicyRef"` carries a workflow-internal actor-policy id. The discriminator + value pair is normative; offline verifiers, exporters, and audit reviewers MUST be able to trace authority back to a single named source without needing free-text interpretation.

kind.description: Discriminator. `uri` requires `value` to be an external resolvable URI; `actorPolicyRef` requires `value` to name a workflow-internal actor-policy id.

MigrationPinSet.description: Four-field pin tree per ADR-0071 maximalist Q33 commitment. Pins the exact spec layer versions a workflow instance is bound to: Formspec definition (intake/data shape), WOS workflow envelope (governance/lifecycle), Trellis envelope (custody bytes), and Trellis conformance class (verifier requirements). Captured on rescissionAuthorized.migrationPinChange and migrationPinChanged records so any post-fact verifier can reproduce which spec versions were authoritative when the case last moved.

formspec.definitionVersion.description: Pinned Formspec definition version governing intake / case-data shape.

wos.$wosWorkflowVersion.description: Pinned WOS workflow envelope version (the `$wosWorkflow` literal of the governing workflow document).

trellis.envelopeVersion.description: Pinned Trellis envelope version controlling canonical bytes / signing layout.

trellis.conformanceClass.description: Pinned Trellis conformance class declaring what verifiers MUST support to replay the chain.

CorrectionAuthorizedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0066 D-1 mode 1 (`correction`): a typo-class fix to a prior record's surface that does not change the determination outcome. The authorizing act records who approved the correction, the targeted prior event hash, the field set being corrected, the reason, and the authority basis. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 §Five-Mode Taxonomy + Authorizing-Act Pair (Q26 maximalist).

data.reason: transcription error in applicant intake; corrected against signed source document

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0066 mode 1 correction-authorizing acts.

correctionTargetEventHash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the canonical event being corrected.

correctedFieldSet.description: JSON-Pointer paths into the target event payload identifying the fields under correction.

items.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer.

AmendmentAuthorizedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0066 D-1 mode 2 (`amendment`): a substantive change to a prior determination that explicitly references the prior outcome. The authorizing act records who approved the amendment, the targeted prior event hash, the prior determination hash, the reason, and the authority basis. The paired determination-change record is `determinationAmended`. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 §Five-Mode Taxonomy + Authorizing-Act Pair (Q26 maximalist).

data.reason: appeal sustained on income calculation; amount adjusted

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0066 mode 2 amendment-authorizing acts.

DeterminationAmendedRecord.description: Shape constraint for the determination-change half of an ADR-0066 mode-2 amendment. Recorded after the paired `amendmentAuthorized` event lands. Carries the prior determination hash, the new determination value (any JSON shape per the workflow contract), and the back-reference to the authorizing event. Verifiers MUST be able to walk amendmentAuthorizationEventHash -> priorDeterminationHash to confirm the chain. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26.

recordKind.description: Discriminator for the post-authorization determination-change event of an ADR-0066 mode 2 amendment.

newDeterminationValue.description: New determination payload. Shape is workflow-contract-defined (object, scalar, array, or null) -- the kernel pins only that the field is present.

amendmentAuthorizationEventHash.description: Hash of the paired `amendmentAuthorized` event. Verifiers MUST resolve this back-reference.

RescissionAuthorizedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0066 D-1 mode 4 (`rescission`): a determination is withdrawn entirely, leaving the case without an active determination. The authorizing act records who approved the rescission, the targeted event, the prior determination hash, reason, authority basis, and (optionally) a migration pin change when the rescission accompanies a chain-pin shift (ADR-0071). Paired determination-state record: `determinationRescinded`. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26 maximalist + ADR-0071 D-3.

data.reason: fraud detected post-determination; entire approval withdrawn
regulatory authority revoked; chain re-pinned to new envelope version

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0066 mode 4 rescission-authorizing acts.

migrationPinChange.description: OPTIONAL composition with ADR-0071 chain-pin migration: when a rescission also re-pins the chain to a new spec-version baseline, this object captures the new pin event hash plus the prior/new pin sets for verifier replay. When present, a paired `migrationPinChanged` record is also emitted.

DeterminationRescindedRecord.description: Shape constraint for the determination-state half of an ADR-0066 mode-4 rescission. Recorded after the paired `rescissionAuthorized` event lands. Carries the prior determination hash and the back-reference to the authorizing event. After this record, the case has no active determination until a new determination event fires or a `reinstated` event reactivates the prior one. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26.

recordKind.description: Discriminator for the post-authorization determination-removal event of an ADR-0066 mode 4 rescission.

rescissionAuthorizationEventHash.description: Hash of the paired `rescissionAuthorized` event. Verifiers MUST resolve this back-reference.

ReinstatedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0066 D-1 mode 5 (`reinstatement`): a previously rescinded determination is restored. Q26 maximalist Five-Mode position requires reinstatement be a first-class mode rather than an inverse rescission, so verifiers can distinguish 'never rescinded' from 'rescinded then restored' cases. Carries the prior rescission event hash, the reactivation authorization event hash (a separate `authorizationAttestation` with `policyPredicate: "reinstatement-authority"`), and the reason. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 D-1 + Q26 maximalist five-mode position.

data.reason: rescission overturned on appeal; original determination reinstated effective date of original approval

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0066 mode 5 reinstatement (Q26 maximalist five-mode).

AuthorizationAttestationRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0066's authority-attestation primitive: a free-standing record of who holds amendment / rescission / reinstatement / correction authority under what policy predicate. Authorization attestations stand alone in the chain so policy auditors can verify authority basis without parsing every authorizing-act record. Reserved policyPredicate values are `amendment-authority`, `rescission-authority`, `reinstatement-authority`, `correction-authority`; vendor extensions MUST use `x-` prefix. Spec MUST source: ADR-0066 §Authority-Attestation Primitive.

policyPredicate.description: Reserved set: `amendment-authority`, `rescission-authority`, `reinstatement-authority`, `correction-authority`. Vendor extensions MUST use `x-` prefix.

assuranceLevel.description: OPTIONAL assurance strength for the authority claim. When present, mirrors the SignatureIdentityBinding closed enum so policy review tooling can rank attestations.

ClockStartedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0067's clock-start event. Pins the clock identity, kind (open enum: AppealClock, ProcessingSLA, GrantExpiry, StatuteClock), origin event, ISO-8601 duration, and computed deadline. Optional `calendarRef` and `statuteReference` capture business-calendar and regulatory-source bindings respectively. Open-enum kind allows vendor `x-` extensions for domain-specific clocks (e.g. `x-medicaid-redetermination-clock`). Spec MUST source: ADR-0067 D-2.

clockId.description: Stable clock identity used for correlation with the matching `clockResolved` event.

clockKind.description: Clock taxonomy. Reserved kinds: `AppealClock`, `ProcessingSLA`, `GrantExpiry`, `StatuteClock`. Vendor extensions MUST use `x-` prefix.

originEventHash.description: Hash of the event that started the clock (e.g. determination event for an AppealClock).

calendarRef.description: OPTIONAL business-calendar reference applied when computing deadline (handles weekends, holidays).

statuteReference.description: OPTIONAL regulatory-source reference grounding the duration.

ClockResolvedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0067's clock-resolution event. Closed-enum resolution: `satisfied` (clock target met), `elapsed` (deadline reached without satisfaction), `paused` (clock halted by an explicit pause event), `cancelled` (clock voided by upstream rescission/correction). Q11 maximalist position: when `resolution = "paused"` the resolving event MUST be present (the pause event itself); when `resolution = "elapsed"` and `resolvingEventHash` is absent, the record represents a synthetic-elapsed emission (the deadline simply passed with no event). Spec MUST source: ADR-0067 D-3 + Q11 maximalist.

originClockHash.description: Hash of the paired `clockStarted` event for chain-replay correlation.

resolution.description: Closed enum -- vendor extensions are deliberately not permitted on clock resolution to keep audit semantics uniform.

resolvingEventHash.description: OPTIONAL hash of the event that resolved the clock. REQUIRED when `resolution = "paused"` (the pause event itself). Absence with `resolution = "elapsed"` indicates a synthetic-elapsed emission per Q11 maximalist.

IdentityAttestationRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0068's tenant-independent identity-attestation primitive. Pins the subject's tenant-independent global identifier, assurance level, attestation provider, attested predicates (open list), and timestamp. Q31-relevant: cross-tenant case movement uses supersession (caseRelationship.type = "supersedes") rather than identity migration; this primitive carries identity attestation that travels with the case file but does not itself rewrite tenant boundaries. Spec MUST source: ADR-0068 D-1.

subjectGlobalId.description: Tenant-independent global identifier for the subject (Q31). Format: opaque resolvable identifier (e.g., `globalid:<uuid>`, DID, urn). MUST be stable across tenants so cross-tenant supersession (`caseRelationship.type = "supersedes"`) preserves subject continuity.

assuranceLevel.description: Assurance strength reached by this attestation. Reserved levels: `low`, `standard`, `high`, `very-high`. Vendor extensions MUST use `x-` prefix.

attestedPredicates.description: Open list of predicate strings the provider attested over the subject (e.g. `legal-name-verified`, `age-of-majority`, `ssn-verified`).

validUntil.description: OPTIONAL expiry. When absent, attestation is treated as point-in-time.

ClockSkewObservedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0069's clock-skew observation event. Emitted when the absolute difference between processor-authored timestamp and Trellis substrate-created timestamp exceeds the deployment-configured threshold (default 1000ms). Provides a tamper-evident trail of time-source disagreement so downstream verifiers can decide whether to trust deadline computations on adjacent events. Spec MUST source: ADR-0069 D-2 (Q12 maximalist threshold).

skewMilliseconds.description: Signed delta: substrate - processor in milliseconds. Negative values indicate processor clock ahead of substrate.

thresholdMilliseconds.description: Deployment-configured threshold above which a skew event is emitted (default 1000ms per ADR-0069).

eventHash.description: Hash of the event whose timestamps disagreed.

CommitAttemptFailureRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0070 D-2's commit-attempt failure event. Emitted when the kernel's commit path to Trellis fails (network timeout, substrate down, hash conflict, other). Carries the target event hash, failure kind (closed enum), attempt count, retry budget, and optional error payload for diagnostics. The kernel records every commit failure so retries are accountable; the kernel-side `stalled` instance status (case-instance schema) lights up when retry budgets exhaust. Spec MUST source: ADR-0070 D-2.

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0070 D-2 commit-attempt failure events.

failureKind.description: Closed enum -- new commit-failure modes require a spec amendment so monitoring tooling stays uniform.

errorPayload.description: OPTIONAL diagnostic payload. Shape unconstrained -- structured by kernel implementation.

AuthorizationRejectedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0070 D-3's authorization-rejection event. Emitted when an actor attempts an action they lack authority for (action policy denied, role-based gate failed, deontic constraint violated). Carries the attempted actor / action / target plus a rejection reason and optional policy-decision back-reference. Pairs with `authorizationAttestation` records: when an attempt fails because no matching attestation exists, audit reviewers can walk the rejection back to the missing authority. Action format follows verb:resource convention (e.g. `transition:approve`, `submit:taskResponse`). Spec MUST source: ADR-0070 D-3.

data.rejectionReason: task assigned to a different actor

recordKind.description: Discriminator for ADR-0070 D-3 authorization-rejection events.

attemptedAction.description: Action identifier in `<verb>:<resource-noun>` form (e.g. `transition:approve`, `submit:taskResponse`, `create:case`). Both halves are camelCase / kebab-case identifiers.

policyDecisionRef.description: OPTIONAL reference to the policy-decision artifact that produced the rejection (URN, URI, or vendor opaque).

MigrationPinChangedRecord.description: Shape constraint for ADR-0071's migration-pin-change event. Records a transition between spec-version pin sets governing a workflow instance: prior and new four-field pin tree (formspec.definitionVersion, wos.$wosWorkflowVersion, trellis.envelopeVersion, trellis.conformanceClass). Q33 maximalist position pins all four spec layers explicitly so verifiers can replay the chain against the exact spec versions in force at each segment. Carries authorizing actor, authority basis, and migration rationale. Composes with `rescissionAuthorized.data.migrationPinChange` when a chain re-pin accompanies a rescission. Spec MUST source: ADR-0071 D-1 + Q33 maximalist.

data.migrationRationale: uplift to AEAD-required Trellis conformance class for new threat model; intake schema minor bump pulls field-level transparency annotations

ProvenanceOutcome.description: Outcome literal recorded on a Facts-tier provenance record when the processor completes (or declines to complete) an action. Open enum: the listed values are reserved across all tiers; vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix to avoid future conflicts. Reserved literals currently defined: `preconditionNotSatisfied` (AI Integration §3.3.1 -- emitted when a capability precondition FEL expression evaluates to false or non-boolean, blocking agent invocation and falling through to the fallback chain) and `convergenceCapReached` (Runtime §10.3 -- emitted when continuous-mode re-evaluation hits the 100-cycle convergence cap for a single triggering mutation).

id.description: Unique assertion identifier within this library. Referenced by assertion-gate pipeline stages (see AssertionUse) to include this assertion by name rather than inline. MUST match `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$` so it is safe to embed in URI path segments used by assertionRef (see AssertionReference).

assertionId.description: Optional stable external reference key for this assertion. When present, MUST equal `id`; authors use it only to make the cross-document reference contract explicit at the callsite (mirrors the `assertionId` property consumers see on AssertionUse). Processors MUST treat mismatch between `id` and `assertionId` as a configuration error at load time (see assertion-library.md §Cross-Document Reference Protocol).

type.description: Assertion gate type determining the verification method applied to the data (Governance S5.4). 'source-grounded': checks that extracted field values appear verbatim in referenced source documents. 'arithmetic': validates that a FEL expression over numeric fields holds. 'range': checks that a value falls within declared bounds. 'consistency': asserts agreement between two pipeline stages or fields. 'format': structural or pattern validation. 'cross-document': checks consistency across multiple documents. 'temporal': validates date/time relationships.

x-lm.intent: Determines the verification method for this assertion

expression.description: FEL expression defining the assertion constraint. Evaluated in the pipeline's data context; MUST evaluate to a boolean. Required for arithmetic, range, and temporal assertion types.

items.description: Field path relative to the pipeline data context that this assertion verifies.

fields.description: Field paths subject to this assertion. Used with source-grounded and consistency types to declare which data fields must be checked.

referenceStage.description: Reference to a prior pipeline stage whose output is compared for consistency checking. Used with consistency and cross-document assertion types.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this assertion checks and why it is required. Appears in rejection notices and audit traces.
examples: Extracted income values must appear in the source pay stub document
Total income must equal the sum of wage, investment, and other income components

rejectionPolicy.description: Default rejection policy when this assertion fails. Overrides the enclosing stage's rejectionPolicy for this specific assertion.

AssertionReference.description: Cross-document reference to an assertion declared in a published WOS Assertion Gate Library sidecar. Use this shape when a pipeline stage wants to inherit a library-defined assertion body rather than inlining one. See specs/governance/assertion-library.md §Cross-Document Reference Protocol for resolution semantics, including rejection of unresolvable refs, conflicting `id` declarations, and hybrid inline+ref bodies.

assertionRef.description: Absolute URI pointing into a published Assertion Gate Library sidecar. The URI identifies the library (matching the library's `url`) and selects a specific assertion whose `id` equals the final path/fragment segment. Resolution is a processor responsibility performed at load time; schema validation only enforces URI syntax. Cross-document resolvability and id-collision detection are planned lint rule G-064 (assertion-library-resolution).

x-lm.intent: Cross-document reference URI into a published Assertion Gate Library

AssertionUse.description: The shape a pipeline stage item takes when it declares or references an assertion gate. Either an inline assertion body (mirroring AssertionDefinition minus the library-only `id` requirement) OR a single-key reference ({ assertionRef }). Mixing inline body fields with `assertionRef` MUST be rejected as a configuration error — the two shapes are mutually exclusive (see assertion-library.md §Cross-Document Reference Protocol for rationale). Consumers (e.g. wos-workflow-governance PipelineStage.assertions[]) SHOULD $ref this $def to share one canonical inline-vs-reference seam.

AssertionInlineUse.description: Inline assertion body carried directly on a pipeline stage. Mirrors AssertionDefinition but omits the library-only `id` requirement and forbids `assertionRef` (mixing is a configuration error). An optional `assertionId` names this inline assertion so downstream tooling can treat it identically to a library-resolved body; when set, `assertionId` MUST match `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$` and — if the body is also published in a library under the same key — MUST equal the library `id`.

assertionId.description: Optional stable identifier for this inline assertion, letting callers correlate it with a library entry and making audit logs stable across inline/reference rewrites. MUST match `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$`; processors MUST reject a mismatched library `id` as a configuration error at load time (see assertion-library.md §Cross-Document Reference Protocol).

type.description: Assertion gate type determining the verification method applied to the data (Governance S5.4). Same enum as AssertionDefinition.type; semantics defined there.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this inline assertion checks and why it is required. Appears in rejection notices and audit traces alongside the assertion result.
examples: Extracted income values must appear in the source pay stub document
Total income must equal the sum of wage, investment, and other income components

expression.description: FEL expression defining the inline assertion constraint. Evaluated in the pipeline's data context; MUST evaluate to a boolean. Required for arithmetic, range, and temporal assertion types.

items.description: Field path relative to the pipeline data context that this assertion verifies.

fields.description: Field paths subject to this assertion. Used with source-grounded and consistency types to declare which data fields must be checked.

referenceStage.description: Reference to a prior pipeline stage whose output is compared for consistency checking. Used with consistency and cross-document assertion types.

rejectionPolicy.description: Override rejection policy for this inline assertion. Supersedes the enclosing stage's rejectionPolicy when present.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosWorkflow': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.

scope.description: FEL expression that determines when this due process policy applies (Runtime Companion S8.2). Evaluated against the evaluation context. When absent, the policy applies unconditionally.

adverseDecisionPolicy.description: Policy governing adverse decisions: denial, reduction, termination, or other unfavorable determinations.

noticeRequired.description: When true, affected individuals MUST receive notice before an adverse decision takes effect (Governance S3.2). The notice MUST include: the specific determination, individualized factual basis, appeal rights, and (if Layer 2 active) AI assistance disclosure.

noticeTiming.description: When notice is delivered relative to the adverse decision taking effect.

noticeGracePeriod.description: ISO 8601 duration defining the minimum delay between notice delivery and the decision taking effect. Only meaningful when noticeTiming is 'beforeEffective'. MUST match the supported ISO 8601 duration grammar (for example, `P30D`, `PT12H`, or `P2W`). As a WOS extension, the `P<N>BD` form (e.g., `P20BD`) denotes N business days resolved against an attached Business Calendar sidecar; statutory deadlines like FOIA's 20-business-day response use this form.

noticeTemplateKey.description: Notification Template key for deterministic adverse-decision notice formatting. Key convention: plain string key into the Notification Template sidecar's template map, not a URI. When noticeRequired is true and an adverse-decision transition fires, the processor renders the human-readable notice from this template and emits a machine-readable `adverseDecisionNotice` artifact from the same case-file snapshot, transition, policy, and appeal inputs.

explanationLevel.description: Level of explanation provided with adverse decisions (Governance S3.3). 'individualized': REQUIRED for rights-impacting. 'categorical': RECOMMENDED for operational. 'aggregate': minimum for informational.

counterfactualRequired.description: When true, adverse decision explanations MUST include positive and negative counterfactuals (Governance S3.4). REQUIRED when impactLevel is 'rights-impacting'.

enabled.description: When true, an appeal pathway is available for adverse decisions. When false, no appeal mechanism is configured and adverse decisions are final at the workflow level.

appealWindow.description: ISO 8601 duration defining the filing window during which an appeal can be submitted after notice of adverse decision is received. MUST match the supported ISO 8601 duration grammar, for example `P30D`, `P60D`, or `PT12H`. As a WOS extension, the `P<N>BD` form (e.g., `P20BD`) denotes N business days resolved against an attached Business Calendar sidecar.

reviewerConstraint.description: Constraint on appeal reviewers. MUST be independent of the original determination (Governance S3.5). Values mirror `AppealRouting.independenceConstraint` in the Due Process Config sidecar so processors can apply a single independence policy across both authoring inputs and runtime routing. `independentFromOriginal`: reviewer is any actor distinct from the original decision-maker. `differentUnit`: reviewer is in a different organizational unit. `differentAgency`: reviewer is in a different agency. `externalPanel`: reviewer is an external panel (e.g., administrative law judge).

x-lm.intent: Closes the appeal-reviewer independence vocabulary so structurally-invalid documents fail at schema validation rather than during downstream linting

items.description: Role identifier eligible to review appeals under this mechanism.

reviewerRoles.description: Roles eligible to review appeals. The processor uses this list to validate reviewer assignments during appeal routing.

continuationOfServices.description: When true, the workflow MUST maintain current service levels during the appeal window (Governance S3.6). Prevents deprivation of benefits while appeal is pending. The exact services maintained are defined by `continuationPolicyRef` when present, or by `continuationScope` as a fallback.

continuationPolicyRef.description: Reference to a `ContinuationPolicy.id` declared in the targeted Due Process Config sidecar (Governance §3.6). When present AND `continuationOfServices` is true, the named policy's `scope` + `duration` define exactly what services are maintained and for how long — replacing the looser `continuationScope` string. The processor MUST resolve the ref against ContinuationPolicy entries in any Due Process Config sidecar targeting this governance document; an unresolvable ref MUST emit a configuration warning in provenance and fall back to `continuationScope` if present.

x-lm.intent: Resolves continuationOfServices to a typed ContinuationPolicy by id — replaces the loose continuationScope string when present

continuationScope.description: Scope of services maintained during the appeal window. Determines what the individual continues to receive while their appeal is pending. Values mirror `ContinuationPolicy.scope` in the Due Process Config sidecar — the loose string fallback resolves to the same vocabulary as the typed `continuationPolicyRef` target. `currentLevel`: maintain services at their pre-decision level. `reducedLevel`: maintain at a reduced but non-zero level. `emergencyOnly`: maintain only emergency-tier services. Used as a fallback when `continuationPolicyRef` is absent or unresolvable (Governance §3.6).

items.description: Semantic transition tag that this protocol binding will match against kernel transitions and states.

tags.description: Semantic transition tags this protocol binding matches (Governance S4.3). Matches any transition or state in the kernel that carries any of these tags.

x-lm.intent: Tag-based governance attachment -- declares which transitions this protocol applies to

items.description: A specific review protocol to apply at the matched transitions (Governance S4.1).

protocols.description: Review protocols to apply (Governance S4.1). Multiple protocols MAY be combined.

x-lm.intent: Empirically grounded review procedures that produce genuine cognitive engagement

transitionOverride.description: Optional transition identifier to target directly instead of using tag-based matching. When present, this binding applies only to the named transition, overriding the tags array for matching purposes.

scope.description: FEL expression that determines when this protocol binding applies (Runtime Companion S8.2). Evaluated against the evaluation context. When absent, the binding applies to all matching tagged transitions.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of why this protocol combination was chosen and what it is intended to prevent. Used in governance documentation and audit reports.
examples: All determination steps use dual-blind protocol to prevent anchoring bias.
Appeal reviews use independent-first plus consider-opposite to ensure genuine reconsideration.

id.description: Unique pipeline identifier within this governance document. Referenced by lifecycle hooks to attach the pipeline to specific workflow states or transitions.

stages.description: Ordered processing stages. Each stage's output is validated before the next stage executes.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this pipeline validates and when it is invoked. Used in governance documentation and audit reports.
examples: Validates income data from the external verification service before eligibility determination.
Extracts and verifies key fields from submitted documents using assertion-gate checks.

id.description: Unique stage identifier within this pipeline. Referenced in logs, audit traces, and by referenceStage in Assertion nodes for consistency checking.

type.description: Stage type (Governance S5.3). 'contract-validation': validate against a contract. 'assertion-gate': apply assertion checks. 'transform': apply data transformation. 'human-review': route to human.

x-lm.intent: Determines the processing behavior of this pipeline stage

contractRef.description: Reference to a contract for validation. Used with contract-validation type.

rejectionPolicy.description: Action taken when this stage's validation fails (Governance S8.1). Applies to all assertions in this stage unless overridden at the assertion level.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this stage does and why it is included in the pipeline. Used in audit reports and tooling.
examples: Validate income data against the income verification contract.
Apply arithmetic and source-grounding assertion gates to extracted income fields.

type.description: Assertion gate type determining the verification method applied to the data (Governance S5.4). 'source-grounded': checks that extracted field values appear verbatim in referenced source documents. 'arithmetic': validates that a FEL expression over numeric fields holds. 'range': checks that a value falls within declared bounds. 'consistency': asserts agreement between two pipeline stages or fields. 'format': structural or pattern validation. 'cross-document': checks consistency across multiple documents. 'temporal': validates date/time relationships.

x-lm.intent: Determines the verification method for this assertion gate

description.description: Human-readable description of what this assertion checks and why it is required. Appears in rejection notices and audit traces.
examples: Extracted income values must appear in the source pay stub
Total income must equal the sum of wage, investment, and other income components

expression.description: FEL expression defining the assertion constraint. Evaluated in the pipeline's data context.

items.description: Field path relative to the pipeline data context that this assertion verifies.

fields.description: Field paths subject to this assertion. Used with source-grounded and consistency types to declare which data fields must be checked.

referenceStage.description: Reference to a prior pipeline stage for consistency checking.

rejectionPolicy.description: Override rejection policy for this specific assertion. Supersedes the enclosing stage's rejectionPolicy when present.

reasoningTier.description: Reasoning tier configuration (Governance S6.2). Attach to transitions tagged 'determination'.

counterfactualTier.description: Counterfactual tier configuration (Governance S6.4). Attach to transitions tagged 'adverse-decision' in rights-impacting workflows.

items.description: Transition tag that triggers the Reasoning tier requirement.

requiredForTags.description: Transition tags that require Reasoning tier records. Any transition carrying one of these tags MUST produce a Reasoning tier record (Governance S6.2).

requireDecisionRequirements.description: When true, Reasoning tier records MUST include decision requirements declarations (Governance S6.3).

items.description: Transition tag that triggers the Counterfactual tier requirement.

requiredForTags.description: Transition tags that require Counterfactual tier records. Any transition carrying one of these tags MUST produce a Counterfactual tier record (Governance S6.4).

requireProtectedCharacteristics.description: When true, negative counterfactuals MUST explicitly address protected characteristics — confirming the decision is independent of any protected class (Governance S6.4).

rate.description: Fraction of decisions to sample for quality review (Governance S7.1). 0.1 means 10% of decisions are reviewed.

method.description: Sampling method for selecting decisions to review (Governance S7.1). 'random': select decisions randomly at the specified rate. 'stratified': sample proportionally across strata (e.g., decision type, actor, impact level).

scope.description: Sampling scope for the quality review program (Governance S7.1). 'workflow': the rate applies across all decisions in the workflow. 'actor': the rate applies independently per actor.
Scope of separation (Governance S7.2). 'sameInstance': reviewer != decision-maker within the same workflow instance. 'global': reviewer != decision-maker across all instances.

items.description: Role identifier excluded from reviewing its own category of work under separation of duties.

excludeRoles.description: Roles excluded from reviewing their own category of work. Actors holding these roles cannot review decisions they made in the same instance (or globally, per scope).

OverrideAuthority.description: Policy controlling what an override action MUST capture (Governance S7.3). The corresponding runtime record shape is defined by `OverrideRecord` in this schema; processors emit one OverrideRecord per accepted override as immutable provenance.

requireStructuredRationale.description: When true, overrides MUST include structured rationale (Governance S7.3). The rationale is captured in `OverrideRecord.rationale`.

requireAuthorityVerification.description: When true, the processor MUST verify the overriding actor has override authority before accepting the override (Governance S7.3). The verification is captured in `OverrideRecord.authorityVerification`.

requireSupportingEvidence.description: When true, overrides MUST reference supporting evidence documents or citations. The references are captured in `OverrideRecord.supportingEvidence`.

OverrideRecord.$comment: This $def is intentionally unreachable from the document root. It is a shape-catalog entry: processors and provenance writers consume it out-of-band when serializing override events to the audit log. There is no top-level `overrideRecords` array in this schema by design — overrides are runtime data, not authoring inputs. The 1:1 mapping to OverrideAuthority policy switches is documented in the description and enforced by processors, not by JSON Schema. See thoughts/plans/ for any future work that would plumb this into a typed provenance container.
OverrideRecord.description: Typed runtime record emitted whenever a reviewer overrides a prior decision (Governance S7.3). One OverrideRecord is appended to provenance per accepted override; records are immutable. This $def is the normative shape processors MUST conform to when serializing override events. The three required fields correspond directly to the three OverrideAuthority policy switches: `requireStructuredRationale` → `rationale`, `requireAuthorityVerification` → `authorityVerification`, and `requireSupportingEvidence` → `supportingEvidence` (when the policy switch is true the field MUST be non-empty; when false the field MAY be omitted). `id` and `caseId` carry the custody-bound record and case identifiers pinned by the WOS-Trellis append surface.

id.description: TypeID-structured identifier for this governance runtime record. Reserved family prefix: `gov`.

caseId.description: TypeID-structured case identifier of the case this override belongs to. Reserved family prefix: `case`.

rationale.description: Structured rationale explaining the basis for the override (Governance S7.3 item 1). The rationale MUST be human-readable and MAY cite regulatory authorities, prior cases, or factual findings. The shape is intentionally permissive — agencies declare their own rationale fields under `extensions` — but `summary` is REQUIRED so the audit trail always carries a one-line explanation.

summary.description: One-line human-readable explanation of why the override was issued. Surfaced in audit reports and adverse-decision notices.
examples: New medical evidence submitted on 2026-03-14 invalidates prior denial.
Reviewer determined original decision applied wrong regulatory section.

items.description: A single regulatory citation string. Format is intentionally free-form so authors can cite statutes, regulations, agency policy, or operational manuals using each authority source's native citation style.

regulatoryCitations.description: Optional citations to the regulatory authorities, statutes, or policies that support the override. Each entry is a free-form citation string.

authorityVerification.description: Record of how the overriding actor's authority was verified (Governance S7.3 item 2). Captures who verified, when, and against which authority source. Processors that verify automatically (e.g., role lookup) MUST still emit this record so the audit trail is complete.

actorId.description: Identifier of the actor whose authority was verified — the actor issuing the override.

method.description: How authority was verified. `roleAssignment`: actor's role grants override authority. `delegationGrant`: a Delegation document grants this actor override authority for the case. `supervisorAttestation`: a supervisor explicitly attested. `externalAuthority`: an out-of-band authority source (e.g., HR system) confirmed.

verifiedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp recording when authority verification completed. Surfaced in audit trails and used to establish ordering when multiple verifications occur.

authoritySource.description: Identifier of the authority source consulted (e.g., role registry id, delegation document URI, attestation record id).

supportingEvidence.description: Documents or facts referenced by the rationale (Governance S7.3 item 3). MUST be present (possibly empty) so consumers can rely on the field's existence; MUST be non-empty when `OverrideAuthority.requireSupportingEvidence` is true.

overriddenDecisionRef.description: Pointer to the prior decision being overridden — typically a provenance record id or case-file path. Optional but strongly recommended so the audit trail can reconstruct the override chain.

newDeterminationRef.description: Pointer to the new determination produced by the override — typically a provenance record id. Auditors use this together with `overriddenDecisionRef` to reconstruct the override chain.

EvidenceReference.description: Reference to a supporting-evidence artifact cited by an `OverrideRecord` (Governance S7.3 item 3). The reference may point to a case-file field, an external document, or a structured fact. At least one of `caseFieldPath` or `uri` MUST be present so the evidence is locatable; this is structurally enforced by the `anyOf` constraint.

kind.description: What this reference points to. `caseField`: a field within the case file. `document`: a referenceable document (form, attachment). `fact`: a structured factual finding. `externalRecord`: data from an integrated external system.

caseFieldPath.description: JSON pointer / dotted path into the case file (used when `kind` is `caseField`).

uri.description: URI of the external document or record (used when `kind` is `document` or `externalRecord`).

summary.description: Short human-readable description of what this evidence shows. Surfaced in audit reports.
examples: Updated medical assessment from treating physician dated 2026-03-14.

pattern.description: Task pattern name identifying this category of work in the task catalog. Used in the verifiability matrix and in audit reports (Governance S9.1).

verifiable.description: Whether the task's output can be independently verified (Governance S9.1).

verificationMethod.description: How verification is performed for this task pattern when verifiable is 'yes' or 'partially'. Describes the independent check that can validate the task's output.

description.description: Human-readable description of this task pattern and what it involves. Used in verifiability matrix documentation and governance reports.
examples: Extract field values from submitted documents using OCR and structured parsing.
Verify income data against source documents and arithmetic constraints.

slaDefinitions.description: Named SLA windows declared against this task pattern. Each entry binds an expected duration to a start trigger and a calendar; multiple entries MAY coexist (e.g. a `firstResponse` SLA plus an end-to-end `fullResolution` SLA). Processor obligations for warning emission, breach handling, and escalation are specified in Runtime Companion §10.3.

warningThresholds.description: Escalating pre-breach alerts for this task pattern. Each threshold fires a notification `beforeBreach` of any matching `slaDefinitions` entry using `templateKey`, routed to the `notify` actors. Ordered independently of breach policy; the processor evaluates every threshold (Runtime Companion §10.3).

breachPolicy.description: Action the processor MUST take when an `slaDefinitions` entry's expected duration elapses without task completion. Composes with `warningThresholds` (pre-breach) and `escalationChain` (post-breach). See Governance §10.3 and Runtime Companion §10.3.

escalationChain.description: Ordered escalation ladder activated when `breachPolicy.action` = `escalate` or when a prior level's `gracePeriod` exhausts. Each step reassigns to `assignTo`; on exhaustion the step's `onExhaustion` policy advances the chain, fails the task, or opens a ticket.

id.description: SLA window identifier, unique within this task pattern. Referenced by audit traces and by other governance artifacts that need to correlate breach events back to a named window (e.g. `firstResponse`, `fullResolution`).

expectedDuration.description: Expected completion window for this SLA. MUST be an ISO 8601 duration (e.g. `P1D`, `PT4H`). An "indefinite" SLA is a category error -- warning thresholds and breach policy have nothing to fire against -- so the literal `indefinite` form (permitted on `HoldPolicy.expectedDuration`) is rejected here. As a WOS extension, the `P<N>BD` form denotes N business days resolved against the referenced business calendar (see `calendarRef`).

calendarType.description: Calendar used when evaluating `expectedDuration`. `wall-clock` advances continuously; `business` advances only during calendar-declared business hours and requires a resolvable `calendarRef` (Runtime Companion §10.3, lint G-023).
