calendarRef.description: URI of the Business Calendar sidecar used to resolve business-day arithmetic. Ref convention: URI into the Business Calendar sidecar itself (not a key into a map), because a governance document may point at any calendar artifact addressable from its runtime environment; contrast with `templateKey` / `escalationStepId` / `notificationTemplateKey` which are plain string keys or sibling ids. REQUIRED in practice when `calendarType` = `business`; lint G-023 enforces presence/resolution.

startAt.description: Point in the task lifecycle from which `expectedDuration` is measured. `assignment` = when the task enters state `assigned` (Governance §10.1); `activation` = when a `claimed`/`created` task becomes actionable; `custom-event` = a kernel event named in `startEvent` (required when `startAt` = `custom-event`).

startEvent.description: Kernel event name that starts the SLA clock when `startAt` = `custom-event`. MUST match the kernel event-name grammar `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$`, which rejects empty strings, whitespace, and any `$`-prefixed kernel-reserved names (for example `$join`, `$timeout.*`, or internal dispatch labels such as `$postMutationRescan`) — those are not valid clock origins. Event-name resolution against the target kernel is enforced by lint G-029.

beforeBreach.description: Lead time, as an ISO 8601 duration, before any matching `slaDefinitions.expectedDuration` elapses at which this warning fires. Multiple thresholds MAY declare escalating lead times (e.g. `P2D`, `PT4H`, `PT30M`); the processor fires each independently (Runtime Companion §10.3).

templateKey.description: Notification template key to render when this threshold fires. Key convention: plain string key into the Notification Template sidecar's template map (same precedent as `HoldPolicy.notificationTemplateKey`), not a URI. MUST resolve into a Notification Template sidecar via lint G-063. The referenced template SHOULD carry the `sla-warning` category.

items.description: Actor identifier (role or individual) receiving the rendered warning notification. Resolved against the kernel actor registry per Governance §10.2 / §11.

notify.description: Actor identifiers (roles or individuals) that receive the rendered warning. Actor resolution follows the same rules as Delegation and task assignment (Governance §11, §10.2).

action.description: Processor action when an `slaDefinitions` entry's expected duration elapses without completion. `notify`: notify responsible actors, no state change. `escalate`: advance through `escalationChain`. `autoReassign`: rotate to another potentialOwner. `fail`: transition task to `failed` and trigger rejection policy (Governance §10.3, Runtime Companion §10.3).

x-lm.intent: Classifies the governance response to an SLA breach so the processor can apply the correct remediation

templateKey.description: Notification template key rendered on breach when `action` = `notify`, or alongside escalation/reassignment. Key convention: plain string key into the Notification Template sidecar's template map (same precedent as `HoldPolicy.notificationTemplateKey`), not a URI. Resolution via lint G-063. The referenced template SHOULD carry a breach-appropriate category (e.g. `sla-warning`).

escalationStepId.description: Identifier resolving to a step in the same task pattern's `escalationChain`. Id convention: plain string targeting a sibling `EscalationStep` by its numeric `level` token (e.g. `level-1`) or optional stable `id` (e.g. `supervisor`), not a URI. Only meaningful when `action` = `escalate`. Cross-reference integrity is enforced by lint G-066.

timeoutPolicy.description: Optional policy controlling what happens when the same task pattern breaches repeatedly.

onRepeatedBreach.description: Processor behaviour when a task pattern accumulates repeated SLA breaches. `suspend`: suspend the task. `fail`: transition to `failed` and invoke rejection policy. `continue`: keep escalating without state change (Runtime Companion §10.3).

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this escalation step, matched by `BreachPolicy.escalationStepId` alongside numeric `level`. MUST match `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$` (kernel identifier grammar). Lets authors point a breach policy at a named step (e.g. `supervisor`) without pinning to its ordinal position so inserting a new level does not silently retarget existing refs. Cross-reference resolution is enforced by lint G-066.

level.description: Ordered position of this step in the escalation ladder. Levels SHOULD be contiguous starting at 1; the processor walks them in ascending order until the task clears, is failed, or a ticket is created (Governance §10.3).

assignTo.description: Actor identifier (role or individual) the task reassigns to when this escalation level fires. Actor resolution follows Governance §10.2 / §11; the processor creates a delegated task in provenance.

gracePeriod.description: ISO 8601 duration the assigned actor has to resolve the task before this step's `onExhaustion` policy fires. As a WOS extension, `P<N>BD` denotes N business days against the SLA's declared calendar.

onExhaustion.description: Action when `gracePeriod` expires without resolution. `escalate`: advance to the next `EscalationStep` by `level`. `fail`: transition task to `failed` (invokes rejection policy). `ticketCreate`: open an out-of-band ticket and park the task pending manual intervention.

id.description: Unique delegation identifier within this governance document. Referenced in audit traces and by sub-delegation chains when maxDelegationDepth > 1.

delegator.description: Actor identifier of the role or individual granting the delegated authority. Must correspond to an actor defined in the Kernel Document or actor registry.

delegate.description: Actor identifier of the role or individual receiving the delegated authority. The processor verifies the acting actor matches this reference when authority is exercised.

authority.description: Type of authority delegated (Governance S11.2). 'signing': authority to sign official determinations. 'determination': authority to make case decisions. 'review': authority to review others' work. 'override': authority to override prior determinations.

x-lm.intent: Classifies the type of power being delegated

legalInstrument.description: Reference to the legal delegation order, memorandum, or statutory citation.

effectiveDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which this delegation becomes active. Before this date, the processor MUST NOT honor this delegation.

expirationDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which this delegation expires. After this date, the processor MUST NOT honor this delegation.

revocable.description: Whether this delegation can be revoked before its expiration date (Governance S11.4). Default: true. When false, the delegation is irrevocable until expirationDate.

revokedDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which this delegation was revoked. When set, the processor MUST treat the delegation as expired from this date forward.

allowsSubDelegation.description: Whether this delegation permits the delegate to further sub-delegate authority (Governance S11.5). Default: false.

quorumCount.description: Minimum distinct authorities required for delegation to take effect (S4.9). Omit for single-authority delegation.

items.description: Principal ID of an authority eligible to contribute to the quorum.

quorumPool.description: Eligible authority principal IDs forming the quorum pool (S4.9). Required when quorumCount is set.

items.description: Impact level for which the delegate may exercise the delegated authority.

impactLevels.description: Impact levels this delegation covers. The delegate may only exercise authority for cases at these impact levels.

items.description: Case type identifier for which the delegate may exercise the delegated authority.

caseTypes.description: Case types this delegation covers. The delegate may only exercise authority for cases of these types.

maxDollarThreshold.description: Maximum dollar value for determinations under this delegation.

conditions.description: FEL expression (Runtime Companion §8.2) that further narrows when the delegation applies. Evaluated against the evaluation context; the delegation is active only when this expression evaluates to true. Same FEL evaluation contract as `DueProcess.scope`, `ReviewProtocolBinding.scope`, and `HoldPolicy.scope`.

holdType.description: Reason for the hold. MUST be one of the seven standard values OR an `x-` prefixed vendor extension. Standard values: `pending-applicant-response`, `pending-external-verification`, `pending-legal-review`, `pending-legislation`, `pending-related-case`, `voluntary-hold`, `legal-hold`. Vendor extensions MUST match `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` (e.g., `x-acme-pending-tribal-review`). Note: `legal-hold` has distinct semantics per S7.15 -- it has no event-based `resumeTrigger`, survives terminal workflow states, and blocks data destruction/retention expiry regardless of ordinary workflow state; `resumeTrigger` and `timeoutAction` are NOT required when `holdType` is `legal-hold`.

x-lm.intent: Classifies the type of hold so the processor can apply the correct timeout and resume behavior

expectedDuration.description: Expected ISO 8601 duration of this hold, or the literal string 'indefinite' for holds with no expected end time. When the duration expires without the resumeTrigger firing, the processor applies the timeoutAction. As a WOS extension, the `P<N>BD` form denotes N business days resolved against an attached Business Calendar sidecar.

resumeTrigger.description: Event name that resumes the case from this hold. The processor listens for this event and resumes the case when it fires — this is NOT a FEL condition polled on a schedule (Governance S12.1).

x-lm.intent: The event that unblocks the case -- event-driven, not polling-based

timeoutAction.description: Action when expectedDuration expires without the resume trigger (Governance S12.2). 'escalate': route to supervisor. 'auto-resume': resume the case automatically. 'cancel': cancel the case.

notificationTemplateKey.description: Notification template key for hold notifications. When present, the processor sends a notification using this template when the hold begins.

scope.description: FEL expression that determines when this hold policy applies (Runtime Companion S8.2). Evaluated against the evaluation context. When absent, the policy applies to all holds of the declared holdType.

description.description: Human-readable description of this hold policy and its regulatory or procedural basis. Used in tooling and audit documentation.
examples: Hold pending applicant response to information request — 30-day window per APA 5 USC 554.
Legal hold — no event-based resume; survives terminal workflow states and blocks data retention expiry.

ruleId.description: Identifier of the rule (e.g., statute section, regulation paragraph, policy ID).

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this rule requires or prohibits. Used in explanation assembly and audit documentation.
examples: Annual renewal of eligibility required for continued benefit receipt.
Adverse decisions require individualized written notice at least 30 days before effective date.

sourceAuthority.description: Authority level of the rule's source (Governance S6.2). Determines ordering in explanation assembly (Runtime Companion S9.3): statute (rank 1) > regulation (rank 2) > policy (rank 3) > guideline (rank 4). Default: policy. Distinct from the 'authority' property on Delegation (Governance S11), which declares delegated power scope.

x-lm.intent: Authority ranking for explanation assembly ordering

citation.description: Formal citation to the authoritative source document for this rule, in standard legal citation format.
